summary.intent: Add macOS code signing and notarization to the GitHub Actions release workflow, then validate the new pipeline by releasing version 0.4.1.
summary.outcome: The 3-job workflow (build → sign-macos → release) was implemented, entitlements.plist created, and the 0.4.1 tag was pushed. The pipeline structure validated correctly—build passed and uploaded artifacts—but sign-macos failed at certificate installation as expected since the required GitHub secrets are not yet configured.

repo: Project uses Bun cross-compilation on ubuntu-latest to produce all 4 platform binaries (darwin-arm64, darwin-x64, linux-x64, windows-x64) — no platform-native build runners needed for Linux/Windows.
Binary name is `context-evaluator` — artifact paths are `dist/bin/context-evaluator-*`.
Pre-commit hooks run the full test suite (1351 tests), lint, and typecheck before allowing commits.
CHANGELOG.md includes comparison links at the bottom between version tags.

code.finding: Restructured from a single `release` job into 3 chained jobs: build (ubuntu-latest) → sign-macos (macos-latest, needs: build) → release (ubuntu-latest, needs: [build, sign-macos]). macOS signing uses a temporary keychain pattern with `security create-keychain`, `codesign --deep --force --options runtime --entitlements`, and `xcrun notarytool`.
Bun-compiled binaries require `com.apple.security.cs.allow-unsigned-executable-memory` (and allow-jit) entitlements to pass hardened runtime codesigning.

workflow: The `gh` CLI output can be silently filtered by an RTK hook — when JSON output appears empty, querying via `gh api` directly or using unfiltered output modes works around this.

GitHub Actions artifact sharing between jobs requires explicit upload-artifact / download-artifact steps; the sign-macos job must re-upload only the signed subset so the release job can assemble the final mix of signed + unsigned binaries.
A failed dependent job (sign-macos) causes downstream jobs (release) to be skipped rather than fail, which is the correct behavior for this pipeline.

friction: `gh run view` JSON output was silently empty due to RTK output filtering — required multiple command variations and eventually a direct `gh api` call to retrieve job statuses.
No way to fully validate the signing pipeline without first configuring all 6 Apple secrets in the repository settings; the test tag triggered a partial run rather than an end-to-end validation.
open_items: Configure the 6 required GitHub secrets (MACOS_CERTIFICATE, MACOS_CERTIFICATE_PASSWORD, MACOS_SIGNING_IDENTITY, APPLE_ID, APPLE_ID_PASSWORD, APPLE_TEAM_ID) in repository Settings → Secrets to enable the full signing pipeline.
Re-run or push a new tag after secrets are configured to complete end-to-end validation of codesigning and notarization.
Verify the released macOS binary passes Gatekeeper on a real macOS machine: `codesign -dv --verbose=2 ./context-evaluator-darwin-arm64`.
The 0.4.1 GitHub Release was never created (sign-macos failed, release job skipped) — a new tag push will be needed after secrets are set.
